label: LinkedIn Job Announcement
scenario_text: Write a short LinkedIn post announcing that I have accepted a new role as a Police Officer with the City of Miami Police Department. I recently graduated from Florida International University with a degree in criminal justice and completed academy training earlier this spring. I want the post to sound grateful, professional, and excited for what is ahead. Mention that I am thankful to my family, mentors, and training officers for helping me get to this point. Keep it short enough for a normal LinkedIn post and do not make it sound over-the-top or cheesy.

label: PTO Request to Manager
scenario_text: Write a short email to my manager, Dana Mitchell, asking for Friday, April 17, 2026 off because I need to attend a family event out of town. I am sending this request about a month in advance and want to make clear that I will have all of my work covered before then. I also want to sound respectful and responsible, but not overly formal or overly apologetic. The email should be appropriate for a normal office environment and should not be too long.

label: Sister-in-Law Brunch Cancellation
scenario_text: Write a short text message to my sister-in-law, Emily, letting her know I will not be able to make brunch next Sunday, March 22. I want to sound warm and genuine, and I want to apologize briefly without making it dramatic. Mention that I already had something come up that morning and that I would still love to see everyone soon. Keep it casual and natural, like a real family text.

label: Funny Text to Coworker
scenario_text: Write a short funny text to my coworker, Jake, about how chaotic today has been at work. We have already sat through three meetings that easily could have been emails, and now a fourth one just got added for 4:30 PM. I want the message to be witty and relatable, but still something you could realistically text a coworker you get along with. Keep it short and natural, not like a stand-up routine.

label: Sales Email to Outside Client
scenario_text: Write a short sales email to an outside client named Rachel Thompson, who helps organize events for schools and neighborhood associations. I want to tell her that our company, SkyHigh Bounce Rentals, is offering 15% off bouncy house rentals booked before April 30 for spring events in the Atlanta area. The email should sound friendly, clear, and professional, with a light sales tone but not pushy. Mention that we have multiple setup options and that we would be happy to help her find something that fits her event.

label: Slack Project Status Update
scenario_text: Write a short Slack message to my team channel updating everyone on where we stand with the Riverside Heights apartment photography project. We finished shooting units 1 through 6 last week and the edited photos are with the client for review. Units 7 through 12 are scheduled for next Thursday and Friday. I want the message to sound clear and organized but still casual enough for Slack. Do not make it sound like a formal report.

label: Software Setup Instructions for Teammate
scenario_text: Write a short set of instructions for my teammate, Marcus, explaining how to set up the local development environment for our team's inventory tracking app. He needs to clone the repo from GitHub, install Python 3.11, create a virtual environment, install the dependencies from requirements.txt, copy the example env file, and run the database migrations before starting the dev server. Keep it straightforward and easy to follow. Do not over-explain things a developer would already know, but do not skip any steps either.

label: Online Product Review for Headphones
scenario_text: Write a short online review for a pair of wireless headphones I bought, the SoundCore Q40, which I have been using for about three weeks. I mostly use them for long work-from-home days and occasional runs. The noise cancellation is solid, battery life has been great, and they are comfortable for hours. My only complaint is that the Bluetooth connection drops for a second or two when I walk between rooms. I want the review to sound honest, helpful, and like a normal person wrote it. Do not make it sound like an advertisement.
